strengths: Clearly identifies three specific, real-world objects with photographic evidence and contextual details
Establishes discernible links between each object and the chosen IA prompt
Provides relevant factual support (e.g., ammonia levels, Clearâs four laws, JanÃ¡Äekâs folkloric harmonies) to justify each objectâs inclusion
Offers a justification of how each object contributes to the overall exhibition theme
weaknesses: Explanations are uneven in depth and occasionally drift into anecdote rather than TOK analysis
Key concepts (e.g., knowledge gatekeepers, personal experience) are asserted without critical examination
Counter-perspectives and critical balance are largely absent
Citations and provenance details are sparse or incomplete, weakening the evidential basis
Lacks a concluding synthesis that explicitly unites all three objects under the IA prompt
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: This work meets the descriptors for band 5â6: the student identifies three concrete objects with real-world contexts, explains links to the IA prompt, and supplies evidence to justify each objectâs contribution. However, analysis is unevenâcertain sections prioritize descriptive detail over TOK examination, critical perspectives are missing, and citations/provenance are incomplete. These limitations prevent the exhibition from achieving the fully precise and consistently substantiated standard of higher bands. Assigning a score of 6 accurately reflects the solid performance within band 5â6 while acknowledging areas for deeper TOK engagement.

annotations.comment: Assertions about musicâs power and responsibility invoke rich TOK themes but are stated superficially. Ground these claims in specific areas of knowledge and ways of knowing terminology to bolster analytical rigor.
No counterâperspectives are considered. Introduce examples where personal experience can mislead or produce false knowledge to demonstrate a balanced and critical exploration of the prompt.

The placeholder link for the sonataâs source (â#â) is inadequate. Provide a complete citationâincluding publisher, year, and editionâto establish the objectâs authenticity and support your arguments.

The student provides extensive procedural detail on fish-care but this descriptive focus obscures the core TOK analysis. Condense the anecdotal chronology and foreground explicit examination of how the experience reshaped their understanding of knowledge acquisition.

The assertion that negative experiences drive knowledge change needs deeper epistemic exploration. Unpack the mechanisms of reflection and observation as ways of knowing to clarify how this experience alters justified belief.

Misconceptions about tank maintenance are recounted without linking them back to the IA prompt. Strengthen the connection by explaining how these specific errors illuminate the relationship between personal experience and the revision of knowledge.

The detailed account of quarantine failures and water chemistry, while factual, distracts from the TOK focus. Trim these technical details and instead analyze their implications for evaluating and justifying new knowledge claims.

The table lists "Sources: N/A," which undermines the evidential basis of claims. Include credible references (e.g., aquarium care guides or expert testimony) to support the fishâcare norms you invoke in your TOK discussion.

The generalization that all unsuccessful experiences lead to knowledge improvement ignores counterexamples. Consider cases where experience misleads or hinders learning to add critical nuance to your claim.

The image description of the book cover is overly elaborate and detracts from the TOK focus. Summarize the visual details succinctly and allocate more space to analyzing the objectâs conceptual relevance to the IA prompt.

Object 2 is introduced without contextual provenance (e.g., edition, reading context, personal setting). Specify where and how the student encountered the book to ground its realâworld authenticity.

The commentary on Atomic Habits recounts Clearâs examples but fails to critically assess the strength of scientific evidence or statistical claims. Engage with potential limitations or biases in these anecdotes to deepen the TOK analysis.

Citing only a generic link to kobo.com does not meet academic standards. Provide a complete bibliographic reference â including publication data or page numbers â to substantiate your use of James Clearâs work.

The claim that knowledge is "usually widely accepted" overlooks cultural and individual variations in interpreting scientific authority. Discuss how context and personal lenses can affect the reception of expert knowledge.

The sonataâs provenance (publisher, edition, context of acquisition) is not specified. Detailing its publication history or where the student encountered the score will strengthen the exhibitionâs realâworld grounding.

The narrative on JanÃ¡Äekâs experience effectively links emotion to composition but stops short of examining how emotion functions as a way of knowing. Integrate TOK terminology to show how personal feeling can shape knowledge production.

Technical musical analysis (e.g., IâVIIâ­ progression, rhythmic shifts) is interesting yet needs explicit linkage to epistemological claims. Clarify how these features embody JanÃ¡Äekâs unique knowledge of folk traditions.

The anecdote about recording his daughterâs last sigh is evocative but presented without analysis of its relevance to knowledge creation. Critically explore whether this habit reflects systematic inquiry or a singular emotional impulse.

The bolded claim that personal experience influences knowledge production is selfâevident. Refine your thesis to reveal nuanced insightsâsuch as how different ways of knowing interactâto deepen the TOK argument.

The exhibition lacks a concluding synthesis that unites the three objects in addressing the relationship between personal experience and knowledge. Add a conclusion to explicitly tie your cases back to the IA prompt.

The header for Object 1 states the title but omits where or when the tank was located. Specify the realâworld provenance (e.g., home, year) to fulfil the requirement of concrete context for each object.

The header for Object 2 names the book but does not indicate its context (when and why you acquired or read it). Detail the circumstances of encounter to strengthen the objectâs realâworld specificity.

Object 3âs title names the sonata and composer but lacks information on the edition, publication date, or the studentâs encounter. Include these details to fully establish its realâworld context.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
